content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Anthony S. Da Vigo 
Deputy Attorney General 
1515 K street, suite 511 
P.O. Box 944255 
Sacramento, CA 
94244-2550 
Dear Mr. Da Vigo: 
December 14, 1987 
Re: 
A.G. Opinion No. 87-1101 
Our File No. 87-295 
Thank you for the opportunity to comment on 
opinion No. 87-1101, concerning an individual who 
simultaneously serves as a member of the State Industrial 
Welfare Commission and the Personnel Commission of the 
Los Angeles County Superintendent of Schools. 
This question 
does not raise any issues under the Political Reform Act; 
therefore, the Commission will not submit any comments. 
DMG:KED:plh 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
./ -
" VI!: 
;'-/" 
{i {" 
"c 
By: 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
Counsel, Legal Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


JOHN K. V AN DE KAMP 
Attorney General 
November 19, 1987 
Diane M. Griffiths, General Counsel 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA. 95814 
Dear Ms. Griffiths: 
Opinion No. 87-1101 
State of California 
DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE 
1515 K STREET, SUITE 511 
p,o. BOX 944255 
SACRA\.1ENTO 94244-2550 
(916) 445-9555 
(916) 324-5167 
The opinion of this office has been requested on the 
following question: 
Mayan individual be a member simultaneously of the State 
Industrial Welfare Commission and the Personnel Commission 
of the Los Angeles County Superintendent of Schools? 
Any views or comments which you may wish to provide to the 
undersigned would be greatly appreciated. 
Very truly yours, 
JOHN K. VAN DE KAMP 
Attorney General 
~Qu' 
~NTHO~Y~~DA ~O 
Deputy Attorney General 
ASD: 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Anthony S. Da Vigo 
Attorney General's Office 
P.O. Box 944255 
Sacramento, CA 
94244-2550 
Dear Mr. Da Vigo: 
November 24, 1987 
Re: 
87-295 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on November 23, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact Kathryn Donovan, an attorney in 
the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
If your request is for 
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
Very truly yours,

7fuff~~cf~ .' 
Diane M. Griffiths 
.. ~uy ~ 
General Counsel

sections.conclusion: This question does not raise any issues under the Political Reform Act; therefore, the Commission will not submit any comments.
sections.question_synthetic: The Attorney General's office requested FPPC comments on whether an individual may simultaneously serve as a member of the State Industrial Welfare Commission and the Personnel Commission of the Los Angeles County Superintendent of Schools.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that the question of simultaneous service on these two commissions raises no issues under the Political Reform Act and therefore declined to submit comments.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of three related letters: initial request from AG's office, acknowledgment letter from FPPC, and final FPPC response. The formal question and conclusion are derived from these multiple pieces of correspondence. FPPC File No. 87-295 corresponds to AG Opinion No. 87-1101.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The Attorney General's office requested FPPC comments on whether an individual may simultaneously serve as a member of the State Industrial Welfare Commission and the Personnel Commission of the Los Angeles County Superintendent of Schools.

CONCLUSION: This question does not raise any issues under the Political Reform Act; therefore, the Commission will not submit any comments.

embedding.summary: The FPPC received a request from the Attorney General's office for comments on a potential conflict of interest involving simultaneous service on the State Industrial Welfare Commission and the Los Angeles County Superintendent of Schools' Personnel Commission. The FPPC determined this matter did not implicate the Political Reform Act and declined to provide comments.
